name: diffdock
description: Diffusion-based molecular docking. Predict protein-ligand binding poses from PDB/SMILES, confidence scores, virtual screening, for structure-based drug design. Not for affinity prediction.
capabilities: Predict 3D binding poses from protein structures (PDB) or amino acid sequences
Generate multiple ligand conformation samples with confidence scores
Process single complexes or batch virtual screening campaigns
Handle diverse ligand inputs: SMILES strings, SDF files, or MOL2 formats
Integrate with external scoring functions like GNINA for affinity estimation
author: davila7
securitySummary: All 298 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterprets scientific terminology (GFP protein sequences as 'SAM database', temperature parameters as 'cryptographic algorithms', markdown code formatting as 'shell execution'). This is a legitimate computational biology tool for molecular docking with no malicious capabilities.
valueStatement: Predict 3D binding poses between proteins and small molecule ligands using state-of-the-art diffusion models. Generate confidence-scored predictions for structure-based drug discovery and virtual screening campaigns.
userTitle: Predict protein-ligand binding poses with AI
limitations: Does NOT predict binding affinity (Kd, ΔG) - only binding poses and confidence
Not designed for protein-protein docking or large peptides (>20 residues)
Covalent docking and membrane protein prediction require specialized tools
GPU strongly recommended - CPU processing is significantly slower

useCases.title: Lead optimization
useCases.description: Generate binding pose hypotheses for drug candidates and prioritize compounds for synthesis.

useCases.title: Binding site analysis
useCases.description: Predict how small molecules interact with protein targets and validate binding hypotheses.

useCases.title: Virtual screening
useCases.description: Screen compound libraries against protein targets to identify promising hit molecules.

promptTemplates.title: Basic docking
promptTemplates.prompt: Dock this ligand [SMILES or file] to the protein at [PDB path]. Use default settings and show the top 3 predicted poses with confidence scores.

promptTemplates.title: Batch screening
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a virtual screen using the ligand list in [CSV file]. Generate 20 samples per complex and export results ranked by confidence to [output directory].

promptTemplates.title: Sequence-based
promptTemplates.prompt: Predict binding poses for [ligand description] against the protein sequence [sequence]. Generate multiple conformations and report confidence levels.

promptTemplates.title: Ensemble docking
promptTemplates.prompt: Dock [ligand] to each protein conformation in [list of PDB files]. Compare results across conformations and identify consensus binding modes.

bestPractices: Always validate protein structures before docking - resolve missing residues and remove distant water molecules
Generate multiple samples (10-40) for difficult cases and look for consensus in top predictions
Combine DiffDock poses with scoring functions (GNINA, MM/GBSA) for binding affinity estimation
Use confidence scores for initial ranking - high confidence indicates model certainty about pose geometry
antiPatterns: Using confidence scores as binding affinity measures - they indicate prediction certainty, not binding strength
Expecting accurate results for large peptides or protein-protein complexes without specialized tools
Running on CPU for large batch jobs - GPU acceleration provides 10-100x speedup
Ignoring visual inspection of predicted poses - always verify structural plausibility before downstream analysis

faq.question: What does DiffDock actually predict?
faq.answer: DiffDock predicts 3D binding poses (ligand orientation in protein pocket) and confidence scores. It does NOT predict binding affinity.

faq.question: Can I use this for protein-protein docking?
faq.answer: No. DiffDock is designed for small molecule ligands. For protein-protein docking, use AlphaFold-Multimer or DiffDock-PP.

faq.question: How many samples should I generate?
faq.answer: Default is 10. Increase to 20-40 for difficult cases or when exploring multiple binding modes.

faq.question: What do confidence scores mean?
faq.answer: Scores >0 indicate high confidence (reliable pose), -1.5 to 0 is moderate, < -1.5 is low confidence. Confidence = prediction certainty, NOT binding strength.

faq.question: Do I need a GPU?
faq.answer: GPU is strongly recommended. CPU inference is 10-100x slower and impractical for batch processing.

faq.question: How does this relate to binding affinity?
faq.answer: DiffDock predicts pose geometry. For affinity estimation, combine results with GNINA scoring or MM/GBSA calculations.
